title: Test a GFCI Outlet
description: Verify a ground-fault circuit interrupter outlet with a plug-in tester.

dialogue.text: GFCI outlets guard against shocks. Dry hands only. Inspect the wall plate for cracks, scorch marks, or looseness before inserting any tester.

Safety gate: if the receptacle is physically damaged or smells burned, stop and tag it for a licensed electrician. If it looks intact, confirm the tester legend and expected light map before continuing.

options.text: Damage present; do not test live.

dialogue.text: Insert the tester fully. Record initial light pattern, then press TEST. Expected behavior: outlet trips and de-energizes. Press RESET and verify lights return to normal pattern.

options.text: Pattern mismatch, no trip, or reset failed.

dialogue.text: Pass criteria: correct wiring pattern, successful trip on TEST, and normal lights after RESET. Add this outlet to a monthly re-test schedule.

Failure handling: do not keep cycling TEST/RESET on a faulty outlet. Label the receptacle, disconnect portable loads, and escalate for repair. After corrective work, re-run one full trip/reset verification.

options.text: Escalate and keep outlet out of service.

dialogue.text: Good call stopping early. Document the fault condition and keep the outlet tagged out until a qualified electrician clears it.
Nice work. This outlet passed functional protection checks. Keep monthly tests on calendar and replace any outlet that fails to trip/reset cleanly.
